headline: Qwen3-14B loaded; measured prompt-cap repair is in the H100 smoke
summary: Run `dftr-1784468746-c1ddb6b8` proved that the pinned Qwen3-14B weights load on one H100 in 11 seconds, then stopped before training because one untouched generation prompt measured 642 tokens against the obsolete 640-token cap. Exact local qualification found all 128 examples valid with a maximum complete sequence of 852/1,024 tokens. Commit `ebb76e1` prospectively raised only the prompt guard to 768; commit `1a88bd7` also completed the matched 4K SFT14/HUMANWRITE14 trainer; and this turn aligned the smoke config materializer to the same 768-token contract. Targeted local materializer/smoke/4K config tests returned 9 passes, with one additional smoke unit not rerun in this shell because ephemeral test deps lacked `torch`. Corrected smoke `dftr-1784469584-5ace5380` is active; bulk 4K construction remains gated only on its mechanical result.

services.name: Sealed evaluator
services.detail: Private endpoint and hidden split ready

services.name: Autonomy coordinator
services.detail: Zero-token 10-minute terminal watcher plus 90-minute safety audit

milestones.label: M0
milestones.detail: Data pipeline and GPU contract smoke test

milestones.label: M2
milestones.detail: 4B reconstruction and 4K lower-variance screen completed; evidence weak and mixed

milestones.label: M3
milestones.detail: Rewriting-first Qwen3-14B task construction, matched training, and fresh evaluation

events.title: 14B model load passed; two-token prompt-cap defect repaired and relaunched
events.detail: The cached smoke loaded all eight Qwen3-14B shards in 11 seconds and failed before its first optimizer step because exactly one of 128 prompts was 642 tokens against a 640 cap. Local qualification measured prompt max 642, completion max 301, and full-sequence max 852; all remain below the 1,024-token sequence guard. The cap is now prospectively 768 with no data or optimizer change, the local smoke materializer now emits that same 768-token contract, targeted local materializer/smoke/4K config tests returned 9 passes, and corrected run `dftr-1784469584-5ace5380` is active. The failed attempt cost `$0.217796`.

events.title: 14B cache completed; mechanical smoke relaunched without download overhead
events.detail: Run `dftr-1784467379-d463605f` timed out only after the pinned 14B snapshot download consumed 16.5 minutes of its container lifetime. The snapshot is now complete in the shared cache. Unchanged H100 run `dftr-1784468746-c1ddb6b8` is active and will test training rather than network transfer. The CPU-only cache-prewarm repair and 4K construction machinery are committed for subsequent stages.

events.title: Full rewrite smoke accepted and Qwen3-14B H100 training launched
events.detail: All 96 rewrite pairs validate at SHA `51f38c80e11193d7a2b57144876d99d3d3e17f8ed39e353230f6cd52702de59c`; provider split is 46 Claude and 50 Gemini, all Qwen3-32B verified, with zero duplicate identities. The SHA-bound Qwen3-14B rank-32 LoRA config passed 59 focused tests and first launched as H100 run `dftr-1784467284-5299ea0d` with a worst-case reservation of `$1.57968`.

events.title: Four-record Claude tail repair launched against the 92-row artifact
events.detail: Committed tail repair `f06dc12` prospectively reroutes only the four twice-zero-acceptance fingerprints to the already-approved Claude Haiku generator, while preserving the 92 accepted rows and keeping Qwen3-32B verification plus every acceptance gate unchanged. Focused rewrite-task tests passed `13/13`, dedicated config `configs/m3/m3_rewrite_tasks_128_smoke_v3_tail_repair.yaml` was bound, and sanctioned run `dftr-1784467203-882e366c` is now active on config hash `ba6554691163d699e13dd625a2b00439533e826ce438dc67a1fecaff41c207a8`. Budget at launch is `$32.267185/$100` API and `$22.252709/$100` GPU.

events.title: Amended v3 smoke advanced to 90 rows; final-six rerun launched
events.detail: Sanctioned status showed amended run `dftr-1784466580-dfc451fe` failed only after adding 48 new accepted Gemini-generated, Qwen-verified rows to the existing 42-row artifact, producing canonical SHA `c615a1d18100bfccc9f872d35f3c79c18f4351247be5cc82eb0e8184890915d5` with six unresolved identities left. Same-config rerun `dftr-1784466818-9d4eaf41` was then launched to retry just those six rows against the same output URI.

events.title: Zero-acceptance Qwen generator slot replaced prospectively
events.detail: All 42 accepted rows are Claude-generated and Qwen-verified; all 54 Qwen-generator identities remained unaccepted after transport repair because of substantive validation failures. Amendment `research_reviews/m3_rewrite_provider_amendment_2026-07-19.md` replaces only the zero-acceptance source-generator slot with Gemini 3.1 Flash Lite, keeps Qwen verification and unchanged validators, and preserves deterministic assignment for all accepted rows. Run `dftr-1784466580-dfc451fe` is active; API commitment is `$32.175029/$100`, GPU commitment `$22.252709/$100`.

events.title: Provider-neutral JSON fallback deployed and resumed
events.detail: Commit `ab8eab7` generalizes fallback only for explicit schema/routing compatibility failures, preserves the full deterministic verifier after decoding, and removes the truncating 1,200-token fallback cap. Focused tests pass `80/80`; run `dftr-1784466296-33f8cb96` is active. The deterministic terminal-SHA materializer and preregistration for the Qwen3-14B H100 mechanical smoke were also committed at `da28756`.

events.title: Portable verifier schema deployed; rewrite smoke resumed
events.detail: The corrected `max_tokens` request reached Qwen successfully and moved the failure into Claude verifier schema parsing. Claude rejected numeric `minimum`/`maximum` schema keywords, so commit `6317995` removed only those transport-level keywords and added an explicit post-decode `[0,1]` validation, preserving the acceptance rule. Run `dftr-1784466066-bc38cdab` is active on the unchanged identities and output artifact; API commitment is `$32.079969/$100` and GPU commitment remains `$22.252709/$100`.

events.title: Incorrect provider stop overturned; Qwen rerun active
events.detail: A manual review of the autonomous reviewer found the decisive transport bug: Qwen3-32B advertises `max_tokens`, while every strict and fallback request sent `max_completion_tokens` together with `provider.require_parameters=true`. The code therefore filtered out the available endpoint and misclassified the result as provider unavailability. Commit `7c2cea4` fixes the portable token-limit field, focused verification passes `88/88`, the gateway is redeployed, and run `dftr-1784465875-9957cfca` is active against the unchanged frozen Qwen/Claude identities. No method or promotion gate changed.

events.title: Post-deploy Qwen fallback exhausted; autonomy disabled
events.detail: Commit `5f60327` added a raw-text JSON fallback after the earlier `json_schema` and `json_object` Qwen transport attempts, focused verification passed `15/15`, and the constrained gateway was redeployed. Relaunch `dftr-1784453338-8343264e` was submitted before deployment completion and therefore failed on the stale worker with the old bare `provider HTTP 404` logs. Clean post-deploy rerun `dftr-1784453394-116e6e29` then proved the patched worker was live by logging `qwen transport fallback exhausted`, but it still ended with `records_processed=0`, `records_failed=54`, `actual_api_cost_usd=0.0`, and the unchanged 42-row artifact SHA `25b311668679cde7d39d710b2c1f9643828c9b5767be8f243174d25e93cc5d8e`. Because matched Qwen/Claude exposure is frozen, there is no authorized next launch without user approval to alter the provider/model contract, so autonomy is now disabled.

events.title: Qwen transport failure isolated; narrowed fallback resume is active
events.detail: The failed M3 smoke was not a stale monitor target. Direct checkpoint downloads validated the v2 artifact SHA `25b311668679cde7d39d710b2c1f9643828c9b5767be8f243174d25e93cc5d8e` and showed 42 committed rows, exactly matching the 42 Claude-assigned identities in the frozen 96-row rewrite slice, with all 54 Qwen-assigned identities still missing. The audit also found the sanctioned `/logs` route was blind for API workers because they wrote outside `/checkpoints/runs/<run_id>`, so commit `d1c8465` moved API-worker logs onto the checkpoint volume and added OpenRouter `provider.require_parameters=true`; focused infra tests passed `73/73`, the gateway was redeployed, and probe rerun `dftr-1784452195-a78ec24b` preserved the same output URI. That probe made the underlying blocker explicit: checkpoint-backed logs showed repeated OpenRouter HTTP 404 `No endpoints found that can handle the requested parameters` on the missing Qwen rows. Commit `d654ba1` now keeps the same frozen task contract but narrows only the Qwen transport from strict `json_schema` to `json_object` plus `response-healing` with a realistic token cap when the strict route has no compatible endpoint. Focused infra tests passed `74/74`, the gateway was redeployed, and resume run `dftr-1784452317-f7c09b8c` is active on git SHA `d654ba1` against the unchanged output URI. API spend is now `$32.075207/$100`; GPU commitment remains `$22.252709/$100`.

events.title: 14B training smoke built while task synthesis resumes
events.detail: The first Qwen/Claude request envelope exposed model-specific HTTP 400 responses and Qwen verifier length exits, so the run was stopped. Commit `23d15d8` removed the incompatible shared reasoning field, added bounded provider error bodies, doubled verifier headroom, and simultaneously completed the Qwen3-14B LoRA mechanical trainer, mixed 96-rewrite/32-generation task assembler, checkpointing, runner dispatch, and frozen gateway policy. All 84 focused tests pass. Resume run `dftr-1784450281-877f65a7` is active on the same validated partial artifact; API commitment is `$31.949823/$100` and GPU commitment remains `$22.252709/$100`.

events.title: Clean Qwen/Claude task smoke launched
events.detail: The v1 recovery remained too verbatim-prone on the GPT-5-mini half, so it was stopped without weakening validation. Commit `fced981` froze exact prefix-stable M3 mixture counts and replaced the second synthesis family with structured-output-capable Claude Haiku 4.5 while retaining Qwen3-32B and cross-family verification. Clean v2 run `dftr-1784449881-7370657a` is active against a new output artifact; current API commitment is `$31.712308/$100` and GPU commitment remains `$22.252709/$100`.

events.title: Rewrite-construction failure repaired and resumed
events.detail: Early container inspection found the first smoke was making progress but rejected many unchanged generator copies because retries did not receive validation feedback. The run was cancelled after preserving 39 validated rows, commit `bfc2e96` added error-aware recovery prompts and larger structured-output headroom, 77 focused tests passed, the gateway was redeployed, and resume run `dftr-1784449586-c1fcafbb` launched against the same frozen identities and append-only output artifact. Current API commitment is `$31.627561/$100`; GPU commitment remains `$22.252709/$100`.

events.title: Autonomous 14B rewriting program armed
events.detail: Commit `90f56dd` froze the rewriting-first Qwen3-14B protocol, implemented cross-provider rewrite-source synthesis and verification, added the constrained gateway worker, and passed 76 focused tests. The deployed 128-record smoke is running as `dftr-1784449129-4005c040`; sanctioned status at 08:20 UTC showed `$0.017771` API cost and no GPU use. The zero-token 10-minute watcher and independent 90-minute safety audit are now enabled for the full bounded M3 cycle.

events.title: Decisive 4K score completed
events.detail: Two independent embedding families and 256 blinded GPT-5.4-mini comparisons completed under the frozen scale-ladder rule. Both MMDs, token L2, human style (70/128), and overall quality (66/128) moved favorably but inconclusively. The treatment had 68% byte identity, 8.6% unexpected-non-Latin rows, and one genuine replacement-character row; the latter triggers the preregistered hard-validity stop. The exact ladder cannot advance automatically to 16K.

events.title: 4K generation artifacts validated and scorer handoff repaired
events.detail: Sanctioned status on Sunday, July 19, 2026 confirmed SFT-generation `dftr-1784440682-c648bc57` and MMD-witness-generation `dftr-1784440682-36bfe848` completed cleanly with `tokens=16384` each, output SHAs `bf91a2418b681e892885f15f5cd05030534cab8c373e66fb1de89f7c8535bdac` and `abedf64c30740b16d28aebaf8ad57d3241b9f91ff96d3c61f8d6e90257588efb`, and signed wrapper receipts. The audit found the production gateway still omitted `output_uri`/`output_sha256` and wrapper receipts for completed `generate_lower_variance` runs, so that handoff path was patched, tested, and redeployed. The exact outputs, manifests, receipts, panel bundle, and prompt sources were then materialized locally under `.operator/scale_ladder/` and SHA-validated. No scoring launch followed because no frozen scale-ladder scoring protocol exists yet for this exact panel/artifact set.

events.title: Held-out generation startup repaired and relaunched
events.detail: Sanctioned status on Sunday, July 19, 2026 confirmed the first held-out generation pair failed for recoverable startup reasons: `dftr-1784439725-1082f9d4` hit `GenerationConfigError: generate_lower_variance requires an empty wrapper checkpoint directory` after `260.644` accelerator-seconds and `$0.169523`, while `dftr-1784439725-6b6ec394` failed during wrapper `git clone` startup after `157.356` accelerator-seconds and `$0.102344`. Commit `87a0681` repaired the generation worker to allow the sanctioned wrapper-owned `worker.log`, focused generation tests passed `13/13`, and the same preregistered comparison was relaunched as `dftr-1784440682-c648bc57` for `SFT-generation` and `dftr-1784440682-36bfe848` for `MMD_WITNESS-generation`. Immediate sanctioned status shows both on `L40S` with `workflow_step=generate_lower_variance`, `status=running`, and total Modal commitment at `$31.180652/$100`.

events.title: Completed 4K checkpoints validated; held-out generation launched
events.detail: Sanctioned status on Sunday, July 19, 2026 confirmed SFT run `dftr-1784436708-b6637838` completed at `2026-07-19T05:15:25.305529Z` with `actual_cost_usd=$0.906923` and MMD-witness run `dftr-1784436736-c94a4507` completed at `2026-07-19T05:22:22.589968Z` with `actual_cost_usd=$1.166656`. The audit found a real pipeline gap: completed lower-variance runs exposed only `run_manifest.json`, not the checkpoint identity required for sanctioned held-out generation. The gateway/status surface now exports checkpoint manifest and adapter SHA metadata, pushed commit `f09dc19` added the frozen `generate_lower_variance` worker path plus materialized 4K held-out configs, and preregistered comparison `M2-scale-ladder-4b-4096-generation-v1` launched run `dftr-1784439725-1082f9d4` for `SFT-generation` and run `dftr-1784439725-6b6ec394` for `MMD_WITNESS-generation`. Immediate sanctioned status shows both on `L40S` with `workflow_step=generate_lower_variance`, `status=running`, and total Modal commitment at `$30.908785/$100`.

events.title: Timing gate passed; matched full 4K arms launched
events.detail: Sanctioned status confirmed repaired L40S smoke `dftr-1784435737-0a9af38e` completed on Sunday, July 19, 2026 at `2026-07-19T04:39:29Z` with `return_code=0`, `accel_seconds=219.304`, `actual_cost_usd=$0.142635`, and a SHA-bound run manifest. That terminal result cleared the 64-step gate, so local ledger accounting was repaired, `M2-scale-ladder-4b-4096-v1` was preregistered, and matched full runs `dftr-1784436708-b6637838` and `dftr-1784436736-c94a4507` launched on `L40S` with `reserved_cost_usd=$4.68288` each. Immediate sanctioned status shows both in `running` state under `workflow_step=train_lower_variance`; post-launch Modal commitment is `$28.835206/$100`.

events.title: Pushed repair and relaunched the 4K timing smoke
events.detail: Branch `swarmy/humanwrite-next-cycle` was pushed to origin at commit `c6af24b`, clearing the remote checkout failure that invalidated the first L40S relaunch. The same repaired config was then relaunched as smoke run `dftr-1784435737-0a9af38e` on Sunday, July 19, 2026 at `2026-07-19T04:35:37Z`; sanctioned status at `2026-07-19T04:36:14Z` still showed `status=running`, `gpu=L40S`, `workflow_step=train_lower_variance`, and `reserved_cost_usd=$0.78048`.

events.title: Two startup-class timing-smoke failures were isolated
events.detail: The original H100 smoke `dftr-1784434111-716a0ed8` failed after `14.154` accelerator-seconds and zero tokens because the sanctioned wrapper-owned `worker.log` prepopulated `/checkpoints/runs/<run_id>` before `run_lower_variance()` enforced an empty artifact directory. After that code repair and L40S realignment, first relaunch `dftr-1784435621-a7cf08e3` still failed after `1.334` accelerator-seconds and zero tokens because the worker could not `git checkout --detach 2ff023680785feb444fd217c6b39de922cc327f8` before the branch was pushed.

events.title: 4K witness validated and H100 timing smoke launched
events.detail: Sanctioned status confirmed witness run `dftr-1784429664-02939bdc` completed on Sunday, July 19, 2026 at `2026-07-19T03:59:01Z` with `return_code=0`, `accel_seconds=3871.837`, `actual_cost_usd=$2.518243`, and a completed 4,096-document witness manifest ending in output SHA `99d8b0ae93fb29ca7d692edd6c025e4310fcd0bf84188c37827e89d8a00eedae`. The exact 4K SFT/MMD configs plus the 64-step timing-smoke config were materialized from the frozen two-arm confirmation contract, then sanctioned smoke run `dftr-1784434111-716a0ed8` launched at `2026-07-19T04:08:31Z` on H100 with `reserved_cost_usd=1.57968`. Post-launch budget shows Modal committed `$20.886991/$100` and OpenRouter spend `$28.483027/$100`.

events.title: Scheduled audit confirmed live witness and repaired experiment logs
events.detail: Sanctioned gateway status confirmed witness run `dftr-1784429664-02939bdc` was still actively running on Sunday, July 19, 2026 at `2026-07-19T03:14:38Z`, only 20.22 minutes after launch, so no stale-handoff intervention or extra job launch was warranted. The real recoverable failure was observability: `/logs/{run_id}` read `/state/logs/<run_id>.log` while experiment workers wrote elsewhere, leaving running experiment logs blank. The gateway wrapper now writes worker logs to `/checkpoints/runs/<run_id>/worker.log`, reloads the checkpoint volume before serving logs, and was redeployed to `https://bassimfaizal--humanwrite-gpu-gateway-gateway.modal.run`. Post-deploy, the sanctioned log route returned non-empty output for completed normalization run `dftr-1784429472-96f16f75`.

events.title: All 4K lengths normalized; baseline witness launched
events.detail: Normalization run `dftr-1784429472-96f16f75` completed in 15.756 accelerator-seconds for `$0.010248`. It produced 4,096 exact Qwen-token targets (median 161, range 88-359) and corrected 4,079 provider estimates. Preregistered witness run `dftr-1784429664-02939bdc` is now active under the pinned 128-token sampling and runtime contract.

events.title: Review blockers repaired; exact-token normalization launched
events.detail: The full independent report was reconciled before GPU tuning. Exact Qwen-token target normalization, pinned/verified runtime, 128-token support matching, incremental witness diagnostics, generic arm resume, explicit generation bounds, and a 64-step H100 MMD timing gate are now committed at `457eb01`. Focused tests passed 21/21. Smoke run `dftr-1784429472-96f16f75` is normalizing all 4,096 frozen briefs; no tuned candidate exists yet.

events.title: Two-check independent-feedback watcher armed
events.detail: LaunchAgent `com.humanwrite.feedback-review` initialized with zero polls and a 1,800-second interval. It checks only for `feedback-claude-humanwrite.md`, stops permanently after at most two checks, and invokes one bounded Codex reconciliation only when the file exists. Training remains paused until that reconciliation verifies or repairs the hardcoded contracts and data-quality findings.

events.title: Final six 4K briefs completed; autonomy paused before witness and tuning
events.detail: Run `dftr-1784419062-7c7e2b47` completed with `records_processed=6`, `records_failed=0`, `actual_api_cost_usd=$0.007393`, and output SHA `0c35745e5a352a63fef17bee246e2c1822cf54a609e0c41e05327754db135d47`, closing the exact 4,096-row brief contract. Before the next GPU steps were launched, `progress/autonomy.json` was externally rewritten to `enabled=false` with a user pause pending independent review of the 4K contracts, implementation, and sampled data, so the pipeline is now parked rather than advancing to witness generation or fine-tuning.

events.title: Safe-excerpt retry reduced the 4K brief gap to six; monitor retargeted
events.detail: Terminal retry `dftr-1784418273-0bb9e6ef` recovered 4 of the remaining 22 identities before failing with 18 misses. Safe-excerpt recovery run `dftr-1784418784-df1cd806` then recovered 12 more under the same frozen config and canonical output, leaving only six unresolved identities. Final retry `dftr-1784419062-7c7e2b47` is now the sole active remote job, and autonomy has been retargeted to that run rather than the stale failed monitor target.

events.title: Brief recovery reduced gap to 22; final retry launched
events.detail: The first bounded recovery preserved 50 additional valid briefs and left 22 missing identities. Run `dftr-1784418273-0bb9e6ef` is retrying only those rows under the unchanged provider and validation contract before fine-tuning.

events.title: 4K brief pass reached 4,024; bounded recovery launched
events.detail: The first 4K synthesis pass preserved 4,024 valid rows and failed only 72 strict provider/validator cases. Resume run `dftr-1784417670-e1bb510b` now revalidates the canonical output and retries only the missing identities before fine-tuning preparation.

events.title: 4K signal path prioritized; 16K brief job safely paused
events.detail: The 16K synthesis job was cancelled after 480 durable briefs so it can resume later if the 4K result justifies scale-up. The 4K job continues alone at eight concurrent two-provider workers and has 599 valid briefs; matched fine-tuning follows its validation.

events.title: Parallel 4K/16K training-brief synthesis launched
events.detail: The prefix freeze completed for immutable 4,096/16,384 corpora. Two separately preregistered faithful brief jobs are now running concurrently: `dftr-1784403322-896f3681` at 4K and `dftr-1784403322-b50767c0` at 16K. The 4K fine-tuning handoff no longer waits for 16K brief synthesis.

events.title: 16K cleaning completed and corpus freeze launched
events.detail: Resume run `dftr-1784387469-9e5b1936` reached exactly 16,384 accepted rows and completed with output SHA `ee8d6f35e11e4e7dbf4b7d11c1c5532b7426d46c42b0ac41867382127ef79626`. The unchanged train-prefix qualification protocol was preregistered and launched as smoke run `dftr-1784403212-e147e7cb`; autonomy now monitors that handoff before training-brief synthesis.

events.title: 16K clean-train timeout recovered by bounded in-place resume
events.detail: Run `dftr-1784358360-4f83b039` ended with `FunctionTimeoutError` after `records_processed=12648`, `records_failed=7192`, and `actual_api_cost_usd=4.919519`. Inspection of the fixed-code cleaner confirmed that it rebuilds its completed set from the existing output artifact and skips already-committed `source_fingerprint` values on restart, so the same frozen config was safely relaunched through the approved wrapper as `dftr-1784387469-9e5b1936`. Immediate status shows the resume run in `running` state; no new protocol, panel, or quality gate was changed.

events.title: Scheduled audit repaired live cleaner status surface
events.detail: The audit found a real stale monitor target rather than a sick pipeline: sanctioned `/status` for active API jobs exposed only the latest delta `api_cost` event even though approved worker logs already held cumulative progress. After adding a log-backed running-status parser, passing 21 of 21 focused infra tests, and redeploying the Modal gateway, live status for `dftr-1784358360-4f83b039` now matches the worker log at `records_processed=10748`, `records_failed=6148`, and `actual_api_cost_usd=4.182616`. No new async launch was justified because the 16K cleaner remains the only active remote job.

events.title: Prompt-brief resume completed and closed the 128-row panel
events.detail: Resume run dftr-1784377434-a4c6dae1 completed in place against the same frozen 128-source config and output URI, processing exactly the three missing identities with `records_failed=0` and `actual_api_cost_usd=0.00468`. Because the fixed-code worker could only reach `completed` after revalidating the committed 125 rows and filling the remaining three, the scale-ladder prompt-brief artifact is now whole and candidate-blind preparation is complete on the scale-dev side.

events.title: Prompt-brief terminal failure diagnosed and bounded resume launched
events.detail: Prompt-brief run dftr-1784376478-461b4d1b failed after 125 validated rows and three ordinary validator misses: two Qwen metadata payloads bound the wrong source fingerprint and one user prompt lacked any source-grounded topic token. Because the output URI already contains 125 committed rows, the same frozen 128-source brief config was safely relaunched as dftr-1784377434-a4c6dae1; the fixed-code worker will revalidate the existing rows and attempt only the three missing identities without opening candidate outputs or changing the panel.

events.title: Recovered scale-dev prompt handoff and launched brief synthesis
events.detail: The scheduled safety audit found a real stale handoff rather than a sick cleaner: completed freeze run dftr-1784360648-7505beeb still hid its prompt-source SHA because the gateway status path lacked the checkpoint mount and only knew the worker's resolved volume path. After mounting /checkpoints on the gateway, backfilling from /checkpoints/runs/<run_id>, passing 10 of 10 focused infra tests, and redeploying, approved status began surfacing the frozen prompt_sources URI and SHA. That enabled preregistration and promo launch of brief run dftr-1784376478-461b4d1b while 16K cleaner dftr-1784358360-4f83b039 stayed healthy at 8,300 accepted rows and 3.180233 dollars cumulative API spend.

events.title: Train-prefix handoff wired while 16K cleaning stayed healthy
events.detail: No new terminal transition occurred. Instead, the scheduled safety audit added a pinned smoke workflow and tests to prospectively qualify the completed 16,384-document clean-train artifact and freeze immutable 4,096/16,384 prefixes with SHA-bound status metadata. Focused verification passed 11 of 11 tests, and no speculative async launch was made because dftr-1784358360-4f83b039 is still the only active remote job.

events.title: Scheduled audit repaired gateway handoff metadata while 16K cleaning stayed healthy
events.detail: The only monitored run, dftr-1784358360-4f83b039, remained actively productive and reached 2,626 accepted train documents at 1.011754 dollars cumulative API spend in the latest approved worker-log snapshot. No new async launch was justified. The real recoverable failure was the sanctioned handoff surface itself, so the gateway and local status writers were patched to expose manifest/output pointers and SHA-bound metadata, targeted infra tests passed 8 of 8, and the Modal gateway was redeployed to the existing production URL.

events.title: Scale-dev panel freeze completed; prompt-brief handoff still needs surfaced metadata
events.detail: Smoke run dftr-1784360648-7505beeb completed successfully in 18.443 seconds and replaced the finished 640-document cleaner as the scale-dev artifact freeze step. The scientific next move is still faithful prompt-brief synthesis over the frozen prompt-source slice, but the approved gateway status/log surfaces do not yet expose the validator's prompt-source SHA/path, so that launch is paused rather than guessed.

events.title: Scale-dev cleaner completed; freeze validator launched
events.detail: Run dftr-1784358360-a40e0584 finished with 640 accepted scale-development documents, 342 rejected candidates, and $0.239342 API cost. The terminal counters were valid, but the cleaning route lacked a hash-bound output manifest, so commit b6a8602 added a dedicated scale-dev qualification/freeze workflow and launched smoke run dftr-1784360648-7505beeb to validate provenance, enforce train/dev disjointness against the raw 26,000-train pool, and freeze a deterministic 128/256/128/128 bundle.

events.title: Validated 27K source pool; scale cleaners launched
events.detail: Source run dftr-1784357254-035e8d3c completed with 26,000 train and 1,000 scale-development records and returned bound train/dev SHA-256 manifests. The scheduled audit found the downstream cleaner handoff missing, added fixed configs, and launched dftr-1784358360-4f83b039 toward 16,384 clean train docs plus dftr-1784358360-a40e0584 toward a 640-document scale-development panel.

events.title: Nested 4K/16K source selection completed
events.detail: Run dftr-1784357254-035e8d3c selected a prior-disjoint 27,000-document raw FineWeb pool from three pinned shards, capped at one record per domain, and returned SHA-bound 26,000-train and 1,000-dev artifacts for the bounded 4K/16K ladder.

events.title: Latest positive-control run narrowly missed qualification
events.detail: No candidate output was generated or opened. Base-versus-human and SFT-versus-human controls were detected and the human-versus-human null behaved correctly. The 64-token-versus-full-human manipulation was detected by BGE but Nemotron returned p=0.0525, narrowly above the frozen 0.05 cutoff. This is an evaluation-instrument issue, not a tuning result; the scale ladder proceeds while a fresh evaluator is repaired before scoring candidates.

events.title: Scale ladder converted to L40S-only execution
events.detail: Observed 4B L40S training processed 140,196 tokens in 493.373 accelerator-seconds for 0.320890 dollars, essentially matching recent H100 token throughput at about half the cost per token. The ladder now shares cached witness artifacts across arms, forbids automatic H100 fallback, and restores the internal Modal cap to 100 dollars. Expected full-ladder Modal spend is 70-95 dollars; the hard 16K stop remains unchanged.

events.title: Measurement-v4 preparation completed; positive controls running
events.detail: A harmless wind-warning row repeatedly triggered GPT-5-mini's content filter. An isolated, provenance-labeled Claude Haiku fallback recovered it under the unchanged exact-source validator. All 128 prompts now have normalized token-unit briefs with exactly 32 empty outlines. Base-model versus initial-SFT positive controls are running before candidate generation.

events.title: Independent 4K/16K/46K scale ladder frozen
events.detail: The user approved a bounded data-scale extension. Each scale restarts from the same initial 4B adapter and uses its own nested corpus and witness, avoiding the exposure confound of one continuing checkpoint run. A hard 16,384-document gate blocks 46,080 unless validity, displacement, quality non-regression, and at least one dual-embedder or human-style signal pass. The internal Modal cap was initially raised pending compute optimization; OpenRouter remains capped at 100 dollars.

events.title: Prompt-brief recovery reached 127 of 128
events.detail: The bounded retry processed the five missing identities for 0.005576 dollars: four passed and one failed. All 127 accepted rows remain committed. The final row is now an isolated recovery problem; tuning artifacts and the untouched evaluation panel remain valid.

events.title: Fresh evaluation panel and dual human embeddings completed
events.detail: The independent 640-document bank is frozen into 128 prompt sources, 256 semantic references, and two disjoint 128-document human floors. BGE-small and Nemotron-8B human embeddings completed before candidate generation. Prompt synthesis preserved 123 valid briefs and is retrying only five ordinary provider/schema failures. Both 4B tuning arms remain complete and unopened; no writing-quality result exists until the blinded outputs are scored.

events.title: Matched 4B training completed successfully
events.detail: SFT and MMD-witness each completed 1,024 steps, 2,048 optimizer examples, and 256,466 completion tokens. The treatment's first-64 median witness-to-uniform gradient ratio was 0.096835, inside the frozen 0.05-0.30 gate; its all-step median was 0.080187. Combined H100 cost was $2.36196. No candidate outputs have been opened. Evaluation cleaning was re-scoped before panel freeze to 2,000 mutually disjoint sources because the initial 80-220-word pool could not yield 640 line-cleaned documents without selection failure.

events.title: Training reached halfway; evaluation bottleneck widened
events.detail: Both matched H100 arms reached step 512/1,024. Because MMD-witness continued beyond step 64, its median witness-to-uniform gradient ratio satisfied the frozen 0.05-0.30 gate. Evaluation cleaning preserved all committed rows and was safely resumed with 32 bounded concurrent Qwen3-32B requests; the blinded v4 dual-embedder and judge path is implemented and passed 104 focused tests before candidate outputs exist.

events.title: Matched 4B confirmation training launched
events.detail: After repairing and testing two stale protocol allowlists, SFT run dftr-1784344275-4ca9ff0b and MMD-witness run dftr-1784344283-61883086 started concurrently on H100s. Both consume the same 1,024 briefs for two complete epochs at a 128-token teacher-forced horizon. The evaluation cleaner remains independent and has committed 50/640 accepted documents.

events.title: Confirmation data and training machinery running concurrently
events.detail: Fresh source run dftr-1784342763-d52433cb completed 900 disjoint records at zero cost. Cleaner dftr-1784343002-37f8a337 is producing the untouched 640-document evaluation bank while Modal generates a new 1,024-row, 128-token baseline witness. The v2 training contract uses two epochs and an explicit first-64-step witness-gradient ratio gate of 0.05-0.30.

events.title: Lower-variance cycle completed with no promoted model
events.detail: The blinded judge completed 1,024 comparisons for $0.607845. Token-moment won 51.6% on human style but only 42.2% overall and was significantly worse on overall quality (one-sided p=0.0463). MMD-witness won 54.7% on human style and 53.1% overall, but neither was significant (p=0.1655 and 0.2681). No automatic or quality winner was selected. The evidence supports retiring token-moment and permitting only one stronger, fresh-panel 4B MMD-witness confirmation before any 14B, sealed, or detector evaluation.

events.title: Automatic result is directionally positive but does not promote
events.detail: Across the fresh 128-prompt panel, token-moment improved BGE MMD by 0.000088, Nemotron MMD by 0.000654, and Token L2 by 0.000392 versus SFT; MMD-witness improved the same metrics by 0.000032, 0.000461, and 0.000858. Only token-moment passed the heavyweight Nemotron effect endpoint. Neither arm passed the dual-embedder intersection or hard validity gates, and 76-78% of outputs remained byte-identical to SFT. The blinded GPT-5.4-mini writing judge is running after a documented output-token ceiling repair.

events.title: Candidate-blind protocol frozen and matched output generation opened
events.detail: Protocol eb690253 binds the fresh 128-prompt panel, 256 semantic references, two disjoint 128-human floors, BGE-small and Nemotron-8B, token L2, a -0.0006 meaningful-effect boundary, and qualified manipulation checks. Token-moment recovery run dftr-1784340692-ee851016 completed the missing steps with explicit volume commit. Modal app ap-neExB7IND2vkCSlcqI9ZID is now generating all three candidate arms with identical per-prompt seeds.

events.title: Two tuning arms completed and prospective scoring path is ready
events.detail: SFT run dftr-1784339215-2799dbae and MMD-witness run dftr-1784339214-065c0f7a each completed 512 steps over all 1,024 examples with valid native-adapter manifests. Token-moment run dftr-1784339216-47c715cf remains active. The candidate-blind v3 decision rule is powered at 97.39% for a two-noise-envelope effect, both evaluator families are frozen, and 21 focused measurement tests pass. No tuned output has been opened.

events.title: Full matched 4B tuning screen launched
events.detail: The immutable baseline panel contains 1,024 unique nonempty outputs with SHA 89c07f95. H100 runs dftr-1784339215-2799dbae (SFT), dftr-1784339216-47c715cf (token moments), and dftr-1784339214-065c0f7a (MMD witness) are running concurrently from the same seed-11 adapter under method contract 0b2a741b. Each has 512 batch-two steps and exact one-epoch exposure.

events.title: Blind evaluation preparation is running concurrently
events.detail: The untouched 640-document evaluation corpus is now hash-partitioned into 128 prompt sources, 256 semantic references, and two disjoint 128-document human floors. Run dftr-evalbriefs-1784338437 is synthesizing only the 128 prompt briefs, while pinned BGE-small and Llama-Embed-Nemotron-8B jobs materialize human-only embeddings. No tuned candidate output has been generated or inspected.

events.title: Full tuning schedule repaired before spend
events.detail: The prior batch sampler prevented duplicates only within each two-example batch and did not guarantee full-corpus exposure. The full screen now uses a tested seeded epoch shuffle: each arm will consume every one of the 1,024 briefs exactly once, in 512 matched optimizer steps.

events.title: Decision-grade lower-variance cycle started
events.detail: Modal run ap-0wrGSlZT5guIFzJuVWqa21 is executing the frozen seed-11 Qwen3-4B adapter over all 1,024 normalized token-unit briefs. The output is training-only and shared by all three matched arms; no evaluation panel has been opened. A first image-build attempt was externally terminated before GPU work and was safely retried without changing code or inputs.

events.title: Complete corpora passed final token-unit and provenance audit
events.detail: Isolated recovery completed the final four briefs without weakening provider roles or exact-grounding checks. All 1,024 training briefs and 640 disjoint evaluation documents validate. Exact pinned-tokenizer normalization repaired provider length estimates, including four values above 4,096; normalized lengths are 91-347 tokens with median 169.

events.title: Ordinary retries exhausted with four persistent brief gaps
events.detail: Recovery passes successfully added 13 of 17 missing briefs. The remaining four consist of two provider content filters and two exact-grounding validation failures. Repeating the identical stochastic request is stopped; the autonomous next step is an isolated, provenance-labeled recovery before corpus freeze.

events.title: Evaluation complete; targeted 17-brief recovery launched
events.detail: The fresh evaluation corpus reached 640/640. Eight-way synthesis reached 1,007/1,024 unique briefs for 0.554152 dollars, with 17 strict validation or provider failures. Run dftr-1784336141-a24176fe is retrying only those missing identities.

events.title: Sequential brief bottleneck replaced with bounded parallel synthesis
events.detail: The gateway now keeps eight independent brief requests in flight, writes and validates completions as they finish, and serializes run-state events. Existing committed rows were preserved. Infrastructure checks passed 79 tests before deployment; evaluation and brief jobs resumed under fresh run IDs.

events.title: Brief synthesis safely resumed from 295/1,024
events.detail: The prior resumable worker preserved 295 validated briefs and stopped after five provider/schema failures. Run dftr-1784331905-e89aa4be is continuing from the committed rows without regenerating them. Evaluation cleaning is independently at 550/640.

events.title: Early 4B fine-tuning smoke launched on 128 completed briefs
events.detail: Run dftr-1784329721-16bf1bb2 is testing one real SFT-arm optimizer step, token-unit prompt serialization, gradients, runtime compatibility, and checkpoint persistence on L40S. It is explicitly non-evidentiary and will be replaced by the full 1,024-brief matched run.

events.title: Training preparation moved ahead in parallel
events.detail: Deployed the strict two-provider brief worker at commit ce7ff7f and launched dftr-1784324765-528d37bd over all 1,024 validated training documents under a 10 dollar API cap. It runs concurrently with the 640-document evaluation cleaner, so only final decision-grade scoring remains gated on evaluation completion.

events.title: Training corpus complete and valid; evaluation cleaner launched
events.detail: Run dftr-1784307700-4a3b8e70 completed 1,024 accepted Qwen3-32B-cleaned documents with 319 rejected candidates for $0.266851. Independent local qualification passed exact ordered-line provenance, 1,024 unique domains and source/clean fingerprints, the frozen train split, and 80-220-word bounds. Evaluation cleaner dftr-1784323983-2f70b1f2 is now active for 640 documents.

events.title: Three parallel foundations completed; cleaner at 550/1024
events.detail: Token-moment and MMD-witness objective cores, measurement-v3, and clean-pool qualification/partitioning are implemented in separate modules. Combined tests pass 117 cases. The remote Qwen cleaner has accepted 550 documents with 194 rejections, a 73.9 percent rate versus 73.1 percent required; runner integration proceeds concurrently.

events.title: Estimator audit completed: K32 direction gate failed
events.detail: Run dftr-1784303078-239a1732 completed 16 nested K=4/8/16/32 replicates for $0.566940. Length-matched K32 gradient-norm CV passed at 0.394, but split-half cosine was -0.01385 versus the required 0.50; full-human K32 cosine was 0.04074. Independent recomputation verified the 128-row artifact. Global-K32 score-function training is retired in favor of lower-variance distribution objectives.

events.title: First audit launch failed safely; microbatch repair verified
events.detail: Run dftr-1784302659-54f7383f exhausted L40S memory at the first differentiable K-way log-probability forward, before any audit result. It used 52.747 accelerator-seconds and $0.034307. The v2 implementation now accumulates the exact mean REINFORCE gradient one sequence at a time; a tiny-model test matches full-batch parameter gradients to 1e-7.

events.title: K-scaling estimator audit preregistered and ready for deployment
events.detail: A new versioned sampler fixes token units and post-EOS reward pollution without altering historical A64 code. The no-training audit runs 16 nested K=4/8/16/32 groups, compares full versus 64-token-truncated human support, and requires K32 split-half gradient cosine at least 0.50 with norm CV at most 1.0. The runner and both gateway guards pass 101 focused tests; worst-case reservation is under $5.

events.title: Independent reviews narrowed the A64 conclusion and reset the next cycle
events.detail: The A64-minus-A0 contrast remains effectively zero, but all 64 prompt sources were also the human distribution references, shifting absolute MMD by roughly -0.0069. Audit also confirmed impossible realized authorship improvement, mismatched power, token-versus-word length semantics, post-EOS rewards, unclean targets, and unexpected scripts. The old panel is retired for decisions; fresh unpaired measurement and a K=4/8/16/32 frozen-policy audit are active before more training.

events.title: Mechanism diagnosis: stable training, insufficient policy movement
events.detail: A64 scalar surrogate variance averaged 17.98 and peaked at 76.78; parameter-gradient variance was not measured, so loss-sign changes are not gradient-direction evidence. KL remained below 0.00118 and 48 of 64 matched outputs were byte-identical to A0. The next arm requires a real gradient audit and fresh prospective panel.

events.title: Reviewer audit preserved the decision and repaired uncertainty
events.detail: All run bindings, signs, thresholds, seeds, and point estimates passed independent review. A bootstrap grouping defect inflated the reported authorship confidence intervals but did not enter the promotion rule; corrected post-hoc intervals are A0 0.325-0.697 and A64 0.335-0.659 with unchanged point separability. Future protocols use the repaired code. The attestation is now independently Ed25519-signed rather than reusing its source signature.

events.title: First genuine DFT result: stable negative A64 screen
events.detail: The signed n=64 A64-versus-A0 report found candidate-minus-control MMD -0.000067 (required <= -0.0018; paired p=0.740) and authorship separability 0.1033 versus control 0.0937 (required improvement >= 0.15). Repetition passed and deterministic factual proxies moved only slightly. Independent attestation passed all 13 blind groups. Per the frozen stop rule, no confirmation seeds, 128-token expansion, sealed run, 14B, quality-judge spend, or Tier 3 call will be used for this failed arm.

events.title: Matched A0 and A64 artifacts completed
events.detail: A0 training cost 0.062237 dollars and A64 training cost 0.071844 dollars. Their matched 64-document adapter-native generations cost 0.097087 and 0.053972 dollars. Every completed artifact has a hash-bound run manifest and Ed25519 wrapper receipt; both output panels contain exactly 64 prompt-aligned rows and 4096 sampled tokens.

events.title: Matched 4B A0 scientific control launched
events.detail: Run dftr-1784279399-17837c4a is training the zero-MMD A0 continuation under the frozen eight-step method. A64 uses the identical method hash but cannot launch until A0 generation, measurement-v2 freeze, and signed independent blind qualification are bound in readiness evidence.

events.title: Real 4B DFT plumbing smoke passed
events.detail: One full A0 step completed with 192 rollout tokens, finite losses and gradient norm, 100 percent unique rollouts, zero repeated trigrams, and a real saved PEFT checkpoint. Actual cost was 0.025310 dollars after one 0.019271 dollar fail-safe autograd integration attempt.

events.title: Training-only bandwidth artifact materialized
events.detail: The wrapper-only 4B producer embedded 256 frozen training humans, computed 32,640 positive pair distances with zero duplicates, and froze five bandwidths. The successful run cost 0.016384 dollars and generated zero tokens.

events.title: DFT re-audit passed; prospective sequencing tightened
events.detail: The repaired implementation passed 65 focused DFT, policy, and backend tests and was published at 3624793. Before launch, a final review caught that joint A0/A64 execution would violate the measurement-v2 freeze order. Execution is being split into selector-neutral matched jobs; no DFT output or model spend has occurred.

events.title: All 64 repaired measurement prompts passed
events.detail: Prompt-only repair completed 64 of 64 for 0.023396 dollars. All requests are unique and natural, only user_prompt changed, and the final repaired brief SHA is b5c8b665. Prospective n64 power and decision gates are now frozen before A0/A64 output.

events.title: Prompt-conditioning defect caught before protocol freeze
events.detail: The 64-row brief artifact passed source, outline, quotation, and 25-percent empty-outline validation. A separate standalone-request gate rejected 64 of 64 user prompts for echoing synthesis instructions. Prompt-only repair is running on a new output while all non-prompt fields remain frozen.

events.title: First DFT implementation held by independent audit
events.detail: The core MMD reward math passed, but audit found rollout/log-prob distribution mismatch plus full-brief, resume, and L40S-memory gaps. No launch occurred. The implementation is being corrected and will be re-audited before spend.

events.title: 63 of 64 prompt briefs validated; one-row recovery isolated
events.detail: Two resumable synthesis attempts spent 0.090966 dollars and preserved 63 unique, source-matched valid briefs. One source ID remains missing, with no duplicates or unknown rows. A strict one-record quote-free recovery is preregistered under a 0.10 dollar cap.

events.title: Measurement-v2 operator pipeline completed
events.detail: Deterministic key, embedding, freeze, score, and attest stages now fail closed on missing power, grid, signature, blind qualification, and hard-gate evidence. The independent BGE-small revision and 192 human embeddings are materialized locally; candidate generation remains closed.

events.title: Public measurement-v2 source pool passed
events.detail: CPU run dftr-1784273680-482885ef produced 192 records from a separate pinned FineWeb shard: 192 unique fingerprints, 192 unique domains, 128 floor records, 64 eval records, and zero overlap with existing training or Tier-1 fingerprints. Cost was zero. The 64-brief synthesis is now running under a one-dollar cap.

events.title: Adapter-native DFT route selected
events.detail: Independent post-v3 review selected a prospective 4B A64 score-function MMD treatment against an exposure-matched zero-MMD A0 continuation. Exact merge equality remains a deployment diagnostic, not a prerequisite for public native-adapter research.

events.title: Original merged checkpoint failed exact adapter fidelity
events.detail: Run dftr-1784272938-82d56296 completed for 0.059438 dollars. Prompt tokenization matched exactly, but greedy outputs matched in 13 of 16 prompts; teacher-forced top-1 agreement was 0.999023 with mean absolute logit difference 0.042519 and maximum 1.101563. Archive and stochastic stages stayed closed. The result is preserved and merge/deployment alternatives are under review.

events.title: Tokenizer-aware fidelity-v3 passed independent review
events.detail: Complete tokenizer-surface discovery, exact metadata exception, runtime token/mask parity, and v1/v2/v3 immutability passed 453 full-suite tests before the live replay.

events.title: Strict fidelity-v2 replay stopped on tokenizer metadata identity
events.detail: Run dftr-1784271369-f403028f failed before generation after 34.107 L40S-seconds and 0.022184 dollars. All core tokenizer files and the chat template match; tokenizer_config.json differs only by max_length=384, stride=0, truncation_side=right, and truncation_strategy=longest_first added during merge saving. Prospective v3 will bind this exact difference and retain runtime tokenization equality as a hard gate.

events.title: Fidelity-v2 implementation passed final independent review
events.detail: Exact v1/v2 config identities, artifact bindings, and all public/private surface tests passed. Four independent fidelity packs passed 106 tests and the full forced repository passed 375 before launch authorization.

events.title: Measurement-v2 independently passed; fidelity remains blocked
events.detail: Measurement-v2 passed a final 201-test independent suite with all historical inventories green. Fidelity's exact artifact and protocol guards passed, but its public-surface classifier missed wrapped private aliases while rejecting legitimate tokenizer keys. A targeted correction is active; no replay, deployment, or provider call launched.

events.title: Both rejected evaluator candidates repaired
events.detail: Fidelity-v2 closed all three launch-guard findings with 196 forced tests passing. Measurement-v2 added exact signed hard-gate evidence semantics with 190 tests passing. Final cross-review is active with neither implementer reviewing its own repair; no model run or provider call launched.

events.title: Independent reviews kept both evaluators blocked
events.detail: Fidelity-v2 was rejected for a v2-to-v1 downgrade path, incomplete launch validation of exact serialization, and a nested credential alias. Measurement-v2 was rejected because multiple required hard gates could reuse one plaintext placeholder. Separate native repairs are active; neither candidate is preregistered, deployed, or authorized for model spend.

events.title: Public-canary isolation resources prepared
events.detail: Created separate public-manifest and canary-state Modal volumes plus a distinct canary auth secret stored in Keychain. The endpoint remains undeployed until runtime/image pins, public manifests, and a real-checkpoint replay pass.

events.title: Fidelity replay failed safely before generation
events.detail: Run dftr-1784249018-cfc946a2 stopped on a strict directory-hash mismatch after 91.857 L40S-seconds and 0.059744 dollars. Weight shards, tokenizer files, and index match; only generation_config.json and train_config.json metadata differ between the original merge directory and later sealed snapshot. A new preregistered replay will bind both identities instead of weakening the check.

events.title: Measurement-v2 adversarial retest rejected six shortcuts
events.detail: All 11 original defects are closed, but deeper tests found fail-open cardinality, per-item fingerprint, power-evidence, inventory-attestation, placeholder-gate, and report-signature paths. The candidate remains unqualified and a separate native implementer is repairing the exact counterexamples.

events.title: Score-blind public canary passed independent tests
events.detail: The isolated private canary implementation passed 34 tests including a real local end-to-end model path. It remains undeployed until public manifests, exact image/library pins, separate auth/state resources, and a real-checkpoint operator replay are ready.

events.title: Prospective evidence-boundary repair started
events.detail: Independent postmortems and a native red-team confirmed the visible human-floor bias and missing merge/interface proof. Public replay, operator-owned measurement v2, and private score-blind public-canary attestation are being implemented in isolated native worktrees with no paid compute or hidden-data exposure.

events.title: Sealed evaluator rejected the 4B checkpoint
events.detail: Aggregate-only hidden evaluation returned semantic MMD 0.059529, delta versus floor +0.029529, S 0.575422, and authorship AUC 0.726914 with CI 0.626166 to 0.828487. Both independent gates failed; 14B and Tier 3 remain closed.

events.title: Sealed evaluator runtime audited and repaired
events.detail: The first true scoring run decoded 128 prompts sequentially and returned no result after 960 seconds. A private score-blind fix added batched deterministic generation, volume refresh, the frozen sampler, and one append-only no-result quota credit without changing hidden data or metrics.

events.title: Reviewer correction passed prospective audit
events.detail: The exact frozen 144 outputs were rescored with repetition as an upper-bound-only collapse signal. All deterministic metrics match v1, the 4B result remains 8 of 9 on no-collapse, and the lone 0.1875 high-repetition cell still fails.

events.title: Three-seed 4B confirmation passed
events.detail: All nine cells passed outline, unsupported-claim, and language gates; eight passed no-collapse against a required six. Mean outline recall was 0.78963 and grounded unsupported rate was 0.03083.

events.title: Confirmation generation completed
events.detail: Run dftr-1784217143-f01137b4 produced exactly 144 documents and 20,809 tokens in 1,966.832 L40S-seconds for 1.279227 dollars. Nine immutable cells were then scored with frozen evaluator provenance.

events.title: Three-seed 4B training completed
events.detail: Seeds 11, 29, and 47 trained for 46,732 tokens each with losses 0.6972, 0.7009, and 0.6960. Total training cost was 0.320890 dollars.

events.title: Three-seed 4B confirmation launched
events.detail: Run dftr-1784216516-91130dd3 is training seeds 11, 29, and 47 on the identical corrected corpus. It must reproduce factual control and pass no-collapse in at least six of nine later cells.

events.title: Qwen3-4B passed the bounded recovery screen
events.detail: All six model-size reports passed outline, unsupported-claim, and language gates. Mean recall was 0.803 for 1.7B and 0.810 for 4B. The 4B arm passed no-collapse in 2 of 3 cells; 1.7B passed 1 of 3.

events.title: Dual-model adherence generation launched
events.detail: Qwen3-1.7B and Qwen3-4B now generate the same 16 fixed dev briefs under sampling seeds 101, 202, and 303. Each arm produces exactly 48 outputs; no 14B scale-up is authorized.

events.title: Corrected adapters trained successfully
events.detail: Both seed-11 arms trained on identical 46,732-token corpora. Train loss was 0.74985 for 1.7B and 0.69721 for 4B; actual GPU cost was 0.056279 and 0.108070 dollars.

events.title: Prompt-only repair and validation passed
events.detail: All 256 train and 64 dev prompts are unique and source-grounded. Field-by-field validation proves every non-prompt value is unchanged. The largest full brief is 1,017 tokens under the frozen 1,024-token limit.

events.title: Reviewer diagnosis audited
events.detail: The reviewer correctly identified the primary defect: only user_prompt reached the model, while use case, style, detail mode, target length, em-dash policy, and outline were omitted. The audit rejected prompt repair alone and required the formatter fix at both train and sample time.

events.title: Directional Tier-1 result failed closed
events.detail: Nine of nine reports completed with exact provenance. Diversity materially improved: no-collapse passes rose from 0 to 4, self-BLEU moved toward the human range, and repetition more than halved. But outline-fact and unsupported-claim gates were 0 of 9, so no cell passed all gates and no larger sweep or scale-up is authorized.

events.title: Directional generation completed 144 of 144
events.detail: Run dftr-1784196454-0822114d completed with return code 0. Nine JSONL cells contain exactly 144 documents and 22,759 generated tokens. Actual cost was 0.887583 dollars over 1,364.672 L40S-seconds; Tier-1 scoring is the next evidence boundary.

events.title: Directional screen reached 96 of 144 outputs
events.detail: Checkpoint seeds 11 and 29 have each completed all three sampling seeds. Only checkpoint seed 47 remains; the worker, immutable inputs, and budget are healthy.

events.title: First directional checkpoint cell group complete
events.detail: Checkpoint seed 11 completed all three sampling seeds for 48 of 144 durable outputs. The worker remains healthy and is transitioning to checkpoint seed 29 with no budget or integrity issue.

events.title: Directional quality screen launched
events.detail: Run dftr-1784196454-0822114d is active from published git SHA 3544b3d and canonical config hash 483d9dff. It will generate exactly 144 outputs from three distinct adapters, three sampling seeds, the default sampler, and 16 fixed dev briefs under a 4.682880 dollar worst-case reservation.

events.title: Real-data SFT completed with three distinct adapters
events.detail: All three seeds completed at 46,732 training tokens each with losses 3.0030, 2.9997, and 3.0033. Adapter hashes differ across seeds, total training was 140,196 tokens and 146.721 L40S-seconds, and actual cost was 0.095427 dollars. The checkpoint-bound 144-document directional config passed 10 focused tests.

events.title: Three-seed real-data SFT launched
events.detail: Run dftr-1784196107-48549e4e is active on one L40S from published git SHA 23e1813 and canonical config hash b57d06ba. It trains Qwen3-1.7B revision 70d244cc for seeds 11, 29, and 47 with a 4.682880 dollar worst-case reservation.

events.title: Combined 256/64 brief validation passed
events.detail: All 320 briefs passed exact ID, source preservation, schema, grounding, split-hash, and deterministic 25 percent empty-outline checks. Train SHA is 498294a8 and dev SHA is 524c224a; the generated Qwen3-1.7B SFT config hash is b57d06ba and the full test gate passed 112 of 112.

events.title: Train bulk pass preserved 248 valid rows
events.detail: The terminal bulk pass committed 248 of 256 rows. Independent partial validation passed exact IDs, source preservation, schema, quotation grounding, and all 64 empty-outline IDs; eight missing rows will be retried for 0.382337 dollars spent so far.

events.title: Train synthesis checkpoint 200 is durable
events.detail: Two hundred valid train briefs are committed. Seven quotation-rejected rows remain absent for retry; slow provider responses stayed inside their timeout policy and cumulative train cost is 0.303997 dollars.

events.title: Train synthesis checkpoint 150 is durable
events.detail: A slow provider call resolved inside its timeout and the run resumed without intervention. One hundred fifty valid rows are committed; five quotation-rejected rows remain for retry and cumulative train cost is 0.225038 dollars.

events.title: Train synthesis checkpoint 100 is durable
events.detail: One hundred contract-valid train briefs are committed. Four of 104 attempted rows failed only the verbatim-quotation check and remain absent for a later missing-row retry; cumulative train cost is 0.153342 dollars.

events.title: Train synthesis checkpoint 50 is clean
events.detail: The first 50 of 256 train briefs are durably committed with zero schema, grounding, routing, or content-filter failures. Incremental provider cost is 0.069670 dollars.

events.title: Capped 256-row train synthesis launched
events.detail: Run dftr-1784193698-9f68c860 is active from published git SHA bc95fb1 with canonical config hash ea354894, exact frozen train source SHA 3884fdf0, and a 5 dollar provider reservation. GPU reservation is zero.

events.title: Full 64-row dev artifact validated
events.detail: Independent validation passed exact ID and source preservation, schema and types, verbatim quotation grounding, and 16-of-64 empty-outline assignment. Final brief SHA is 524c224a; all dev synthesis and recovery calls cost 0.190714 dollars.

events.title: One provider-policy row recovered safely
events.detail: GPT-5-mini consistently filtered one benign governing-board campaign page. A preregistered pinned Claude Haiku 4.5 fallback ran only for that missing row with a neutral non-persuasive prompt, passed the unchanged contract, and cost 0.001930 dollars.

events.title: Strict-schema dev synthesis resumed
events.detail: A one-record transport smoke independently passed source preservation, required types, non-empty outline, facts, and verbatim quotation grounding for 0.001711 dollars. The unsupported temperature field was removed and the original 64-record dev job is active as dftr-1784192598-f958d49d under its unchanged 2 dollar cap.

events.title: Malformed provider output contained and repaired
events.detail: Attempt 1 stopped after 10 of 12 records failed basic JSON transport, costing 0.069022 dollars; attempt 2 revealed an incompatible route constraint and was cancelled before billing. No malformed row entered training and the train gate stayed closed.

events.title: Real-data pilot source reproduced and frozen
events.detail: Attempts 3 and 4 each produced exactly 320 records with identical train SHA 3884fdf0, dev SHA 7012e7a8, and manifest SHA 6b264d07. Independent validation confirmed 256/64 counts, 320 unique domains, and zero overlap with all published exclusions at zero GPU/provider spend.

events.title: Recovery pipeline prepared through SFT admission
events.detail: The outage window was used to complete fail-closed brief validation, source/brief provenance checks, synthesis config generation, and a separate hash-bound 256/64 Qwen3-1.7B training path without weakening the original fixture contract. Combined data and experiment tests pass 31/31; no provider or GPU reservation exists.

events.title: Pilot source attempt stopped by Hugging Face outage
events.detail: The new constrained source job was accepted with exact published provenance and a zero-dollar reservation, then failed before selecting records when both local and Modal routes timed out against Hugging Face. No data source was substituted and no paid call occurred; retry cadence is lengthened until the official service status recovers.

events.title: Credential-safe real-data path deployed
events.detail: The gateway now supports preregistered, fully pinned FineWeb source materialization directly to the checkpoint volume without exposing the Hugging Face credential. All 16 infrastructure tests pass and the 320-document pilot excludes all 40 published M0/Tier-1 fingerprints and domains.

events.title: Full Tier-1 screen failed closed
events.detail: All 45 reports are complete and hash-valid, but no sampler passed every hard gate: human-calibrated no-collapse was 0/45 and unsupported-claim non-inferiority was 18/45. The preregistered freeze selected nothing. Provider spend is 0.039521 dollars and GPU commitment remains 0.683574 dollars; no scale-up is justified.

events.title: Independent Tier-1 human bank published
events.detail: A preregistered public FineWeb bank now provides 32 visible human documents from 32 domains, with zero overlap against every M0 train/dev fingerprint. Two complete materialization passes reproduced bank SHA ebcff5bc byte-for-byte; the hidden Tier-2 split remains sealed.
